refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup link to maintain availability.
FR4: Log link availability data for verification.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Real-time link health monitoring and alerting system.
DP3: Link recovery protocols and error correction mechanisms.
DP4: Data logging and analytics for link performance.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover logic
Recovery protocols depend on network link status
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically supports the FR with manageable coupling; monitoring and recovery are integral but can be modularized.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundant network infrastructure is feasible within system constraints.
Risk of increased system complexity impacting safety-critical operations.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing monitoring and recovery DPs separately if coupling causes integration complexity.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: All teleoperation link management including redundancy, failover, and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections for teleoperation data.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Status and health data of the teleoperation link sent to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control inputs received for teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but teleoperation link specifics are diffused.
Vehicle module depends on link but does not own link management.
Primary owner of teleoperation link availability functions.
Safety monitors alerts but does not manage link.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Teleoperation module should own the link availability DP to centralize control and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Integration complexity with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure robust interface definitions and fail-safe mechanisms between Teleoperation and other modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR and supports the availability target.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion of link status data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services may complicate ownership.
Failover logic tightly coupled with Teleoperation module but requires clear interface boundaries.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has high containment score and low diffusion score, indicating good ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics via Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FR to Teleoperation IM with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit fail-safe interface protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
Consider backup ownership or redundancy within Teleoperation module to mitigate single point of failure.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the requirement with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and ownership are feasible with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup link to maintain availability.
FR4: Log link availability data for verification.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Redundant network infrastructure is feasible and supported by Teleoperation module.
Interfaces between modules are well-defined and stable.
open_risks: Single point of failure in Teleoperation module.
Integration complexity with Safety Services and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Link availability measurement data over evaluation period.
Interface compliance and failover test results.
